name: Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species
description: Raman spectra and composition data for titration experiments of various acids in aqueous solution. Includes acetic, citric, formic, itaconic, levulinic, oxalic, and succinic acids. Data for concentration monitoring and indirect hard modeling.
citeAs: @article{Echtermeyer_2020, title={Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species}, volume={75}, ISSN={1943-3530}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1177/0003702820973275}, DOI={10.1177/0003702820973275}, number={5}, journal={Applied Spectroscopy}, publisher={SAGE Publications}, author={Echtermeyer, Alexander and Marks, Caroline and Mitsos, Alexander and Viell, Jorn}, year={2020}, month=dec, pages={506--519}}
citation: Echtermeyer, Alexander Walter Wilhelm; Marks, Caroline; Mitsos, Alexander; Viell, Jörn. Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species. Applied Spectroscopy, 2021, 75(5):506–519. DOI: 10.1177/0003702820973275.

distribution.name: Inline Raman Spectroscopy and Indirect Hard Modeling for Concentration Monitoring of Dissociated Acid Species
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Target value(s): concentration, physical property, or other continuous measurement.

rai:dataCollection: Inline Raman spectra acquired during NaOH (1 mol L⁻¹) titration of acetic acid (Merck, 99–100%) in deionized water (0.8 μS cm⁻¹) at 25 °C using a Kaiser RXN2 Raman analyzer (400 mW, 785 nm) with NIR fiber-optic immersion probe, spectral range 160–3285 cm⁻¹, 4 cm⁻¹ resolution, 60 s acquisition time. Ambient light excluded by non-transparent black PVC liner. pH monitored continuously with SenTix 940 electrode and Multil 3420 pH meter (WTW).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Standard Normal Variate (SNV) normalisation, linear baseline correction, and offset subtraction applied. Evaluation range 1025–1850 cm⁻¹ selected, excluding atmospheric O₂ signal (1545–1565 cm⁻¹). Preprocessing performed in MATLAB R2017a (spectral subtraction) and PEAXACT ver. 4.5 (S-PACT).
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Mole fraction targets for two acid species (AA, AA⁻) derived from pH-based acid dissociation equilibria combined with Indirect Hard Modeling (IHM). Method A uses literature pKa values; method B estimates pKa = 4.70 via MCR-ALS and IHM fitting (close to literature). Calibration validated with leave-10%-out cross-validation; RMSECV ≤ 2.43 × 10⁻⁴ mol mol⁻¹.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information is included. All spectra originate from chemical samples without individual human identifiers.
rai:dataBiases: Spectra collected under ideal dilute mixture assumption (activity coefficients = 1), which introduces systematic error at ionic strengths above 0.01 mol L⁻¹. Data acquired at a single instrument (Kaiser RXN2, 785 nm) and temperature (25 °C); models may not transfer without recalibration. Monoprotic system covers only two species (AA, AA⁻).
rai:dataLimitations: Models calibrated on NaOH titration in deionized water at 25 °C; not directly applicable to complex matrices, elevated temperatures, or concentrations outside the calibration range. Ideal mixture assumption not strictly valid at concentrations above 0.1 mol L⁻¹. Extrapolation to other instruments requires recalibration.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Acetic acid is a key platform chemical in biorefinery, fermentation, and chemical manufacturing. Inline quantification of its dissociation species supports real-time monitoring and control of biotransformation and acid purification processes, contributing to more efficient biobased production.
rai:dataUseCases: Inline concentration monitoring of monoprotic acid dissociation in aqueous solution; IHM vs. PLS benchmarking; pKa estimation for systems with unknown dissociation constants; process analytical technology (PAT) in biobased acid production and purification.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Data collection: Laboratory measurements on chemical samples including pure compounds, mixtures, and solutions. Preprocessing: Spectra may have undergone baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation, and wavenumber calibration. Exact steps are described in the accompanying publication. Annotation: Ground-truth target values measured by validated reference analytical methods (e.g., enzymatic assays, dynamic light scattering, gas chromatography, or DFT computation).
